do_not: Do not add a meaningless self-assignment.
diagnostics.message: Variable 'enabled' is read but never written by any action or transition effect.

recommended_actions.rationale: Add the intended update if the variable should change.
Replace the read with a literal if the value is intentionally constant.

repair_guidance: This is a static dataflow warning about missing writes after initialization.
Do not add a self-assignment or dummy update only to silence the warning.
diagnostics.summary: The variable is used as input but never updated after its initial definition, so model behavior may be accidentally constant.

do_not: Do not add a meaningless self-assignment; it does not model a real change.
Do not rewrite the guard to a meaningless constant only to silence this diagnostic.
diagnostics.message: Transition guard reads only variables that are never changed by actions or effects.

recommended_actions.rationale: Add the missing state update if the guard should evolve at runtime.
Simplify or remove the guard if the initial value is intentional.

repair_guidance: This is a static dataflow warning, not a proof that the guard is satisfiable.
Adding a write is useful only when the variable should genuinely evolve at runtime.
diagnostics.summary: This guard is controlled only by initial variable values. If those values never change, the transition condition is effectively fixed.

do_not: Do not add a meaningless event just to silence this info.
diagnostics.message: Transition 'Root.Fault' -> 'Root.Shutdown' has no event or guard.

recommended_actions.rationale: Keep it when the state should immediately advance.
Add the missing event or guard if the transition should wait.

diagnostics.summary: The transition is not event-triggered and has no guard. This may be a deliberate unconditional fall-through, so treat it as context rather than a fix target.

do_not: Do not add a meaningless event just to silence this info.
diagnostics.message: Transition 'Root.Shutdown' -> '[*]' has no event or guard.

recommended_actions.rationale: Keep it when the state should immediately advance.
Add the missing event or guard if the transition should wait.

diagnostics.summary: The transition is not event-triggered and has no guard. This may be a deliberate unconditional fall-through, so treat it as context rather than a fix target.

do_not: Do not replace the guard with true unless the transition really should become unconditional.
Do not confuse this SMT finding with W_GUARD_CONST_FALSE; variables and runtime-definedness matter here.
diagnostics.message: A transition guard is unsatisfiable under model variable type and runtime-definedness constraints.

recommended_actions.rationale: Relax or correct the contradictory guard condition.
Remove the transition if the guard intentionally makes it unreachable.

repair_guidance: This is verify-backed: SMT proved the guard unsatisfiable under model constraints.
Prefer correcting the contradictory guard over making the transition unconditional.
If the same variable also has static dataflow warnings, the repair must address both the contradiction and the missing guard-affecting data flow.
diagnostics.summary: SMT proves the guard cannot become true for any valid variable valuation. This differs from W_GUARD_CONST_FALSE, which only reports literal-only guards folded syntactically to false.

repair_protocol.goal: repair the FCSTM model with the smallest semantic source change
rules: Make the smallest source edit that preserves the modeler's apparent intent.
Use diagnostic source/provenance before choosing a fix; inspect-static warnings are not solver proofs.
Do not mechanically stack all suggested actions when multiple diagnostics refer to the same region.
Do not delete states or transitions unless the report explicitly says the element is unused and the design intent supports deletion.
A repair should clear all error and warning diagnostics; info diagnostics may remain when the model intent supports them.
If changing a guard into an event-only transition makes a variable declaration unused, remove that variable or keep a real guard-affecting data-flow path.
